documents.title: Lofi / Lo-Fi Hip-Hop
documents.content: Lofi (lo-fi hip-hop) features mellow beats, soft melodies, and a nostalgic, laid-back atmosphere. Typical energy: 0.2–0.5. Best for studying, focus, and relaxed evenings. Often instrumental or minimal vocals. Acoustic textures common.

documents.title: Pop
documents.content: Pop music is broadly appealing, driven by catchy melodies, hooks, and modern production. Energy varies widely (0.5–0.95). Typical moods: happy, uplifting, energetic. Often features strong vocals and dance-friendly rhythms.

documents.title: Rock / Alternative Rock / Indie Rock
documents.content: Rock music is guitar-driven with powerful drums and bass. High energy (0.7–0.95). Moods: intense, moody, melancholic. Great for workouts, driving, or intense focus. Alt rock is more experimental; indie rock has an independent label aesthetic.

documents.title: Jazz
documents.content: Jazz is improvisational and harmonically rich. Typical energy: 0.2–0.5. Moods: relaxed, sophisticated, warm. Ideal for dinner, late-night, or background listening. Usually acoustic instruments: piano, saxophone, upright bass, drums.

documents.title: Ambient / Atmospheric
documents.content: Ambient music creates soundscapes with minimal structure and very low energy (0.1–0.4). Mood: chill, meditative, dreamy. Good for sleep, meditation, and background focus. Highly instrumental with no traditional song structure.

documents.title: Synthwave / Retro Electronic
documents.content: Synthwave is 80s-inspired electronic music with synthesizers, drum machines, and a retro-futuristic feel. Energy: 0.6–0.85. Mood: moody, nostalgic, cinematic. Great for night drives, gaming, or focused work.

documents.title: Folk / Acoustic Singer-Songwriter
documents.content: Folk music features acoustic instruments (guitar, banjo, fiddle), storytelling lyrics, and an organic sound. Low-to-medium energy (0.3–0.6). Moods: melancholic, nostalgic, relaxed. Often clean and lyric-focused.

documents.title: Hip-Hop / Rap
documents.content: Hip-hop features rhythmic vocal delivery over beats. Energy: 0.7–0.95. Moods: intense, confident, uplifting. Often has explicit content. Great for workouts, parties, and hype. Trap is a modern sub-genre with heavy bass and hi-hats.

documents.title: Study / Focus / Coding Music
documents.content: Study music should have low energy (0.2–0.45), be non-distracting, and often instrumental. Best genres: lofi, ambient, jazz. Mood: focused, chill. Avoid explicit lyrics, high energy, or powerful vocals that pull attention away.

documents.title: Workout / Gym / Exercise Music
documents.content: Workout music needs high energy (0.8–1.0), fast tempo (130+ BPM), and motivating vibes. Best genres: rock, hip-hop, pop, electronic. Mood: intense, energetic. Helps maintain exercise intensity and push through fatigue.

documents.title: Chill / Unwind / Relaxation Music
documents.content: Chill music has low-to-medium energy (0.2–0.55) and a laid-back, easygoing vibe. Best genres: lofi, jazz, indie, ambient. Mood: chill, relaxed. Good for unwinding after work, casual listening, or background during light tasks.

documents.title: Party / Dance / Club Music
documents.content: Party music is high energy (0.75–1.0) with strong beats and dance-friendly rhythm. Best genres: pop, hip-hop, electronic. Mood: happy, energetic, uplifting. High danceability and valence are key. Great for social settings.

documents.title: Sad / Melancholic / Rainy Day Music
documents.content: Sad or emotional music has low valence (minor key), introspective quality, and varied energy. Best genres: folk, indie rock, alternative. Moods: melancholic, moody. Good for processing emotions, rainy days, late-night reflection.

documents.title: Morning / Energizing / Sunrise Music
documents.content: Morning music is uplifting and energizing to start the day. Medium-to-high energy (0.6–0.9). Best genres: pop, indie pop, synthwave. Moods: happy, uplifting, energetic. Positive valence and a bright, forward-moving sound.

documents.title: 1990s Music
documents.content: The 1990s saw grunge, alternative rock, R&B, and early hip-hop dominate. Key artists: Nirvana, Radiohead, Neil Young. Characteristic sound: raw, guitar-heavy, emotionally direct. Decade value: 1990.

documents.title: 2000s Music
documents.content: The 2000s featured indie rock, pop-punk, and R&B crossovers. Key artists: Coldplay, The Strokes. Era of iTunes and digital downloads. Often polished production with emotional, melodic writing. Decade value: 2000.

documents.title: 2010s Music
documents.content: The 2010s were defined by streaming, EDM, trap, and pop crossovers. Artists: The Weeknd, Dua Lipa, Travis Scott. High production quality and digital sound design. Decade value: 2010.

documents.title: 2020s Music
documents.content: The 2020s feature lofi revival, bedroom pop, and continued streaming dominance. TikTok-driven micro-trends. More DIY production aesthetics alongside polished pop releases. Decade value: 2020.
